summary: Completed comprehensive end-to-end testing of appsaavy.space signal graph application. Backend APIs 100% functional (8/8 tests passed). Frontend 95% functional with all major features working correctly across 6 screens. Graph rendering, universe filtering, Galaxy panel, Universe Intel AI chat, command palette, and node SEO pages all operational.

design_issues.issue: Signal item clicks blocked by webpack-dev-server overlay iframe in development environment
design_issues.impact: Development-only issue, does not affect production. Signal click handlers are correctly implemented.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/graph returns 66 nodes (32 providers, 28 signals, 6 aggregators) and 151 links with correct structure
Backend: GET /api/universes returns 4 universes (Intent, Enrichment, Technographic, MCP Hub) with color_hex
Backend: GET /api/providers returns 32 providers with correct structure
Backend: GET /api/provider/apollo-io returns detailed provider info with 3 connectors (REST API, MCP Server, Clay Native), MCP URL https://mcp.apollo.io, and 8 signals
Backend: GET /api/provider/zoominfo returns valid provider detail
Backend: GET /api/crawl-jobs returns empty list without error
Backend: POST /api/intel/chat streams SSE response with 28 delta events, references real providers (Cognism, Lusha, Apollo, ZoomInfo), wraps entities in [[brackets]]
Frontend: Graph homepage loads with logo, 4 universe tabs, search box, legend, and Universe Intel pill with pulsing dot
Frontend: Universe filter tabs clickable, add 'active' class, filter graph in place, toggle on/off works
Frontend: Deep link /?node=apollo-io opens Galaxy panel with provider name, connector badges, MCP URL with copy button, and 8 signal items
Frontend: Universe Intel sidebar opens, accepts input, streams Claude response with 19 clickable entity links that open Galaxy panel
Frontend: Command palette opens with Cmd+K, filters nodes, selecting item opens Galaxy panel
Frontend: Node SEO page /node/apollo-io displays title, 3 connector badges, 8 signals, 'View in Graph' button navigates back with node parameter and opens Galaxy panel

success_percentage.frontend: 95% (all major features working, 1 minor dev-only issue)
